Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, elevated creatinine, and low CO₂, suggesting possible chronic kidney dysfunction and metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating renal impairment with possible nephrotic features and tubular dysfunction. No evidence of urinary infection. Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.
